tasks.name: T1: HR app scaffold + Employee model + ViewSet (list/retrieve/update) + URL routing
tasks.description: Create the apps.hr Django app foundation. Adds Employee model (OneToOneField to User) with hire_date, employment_status (active/leave/terminated), contract_type (clt/pj/estagio/temporary), terminated_at (nullable), created_at, updated_at. Generates migration that is reversible and concurrent-safe. Adds 'rh' to ALLOWED_MODULE_KEYS in apps/core/constants.py. Registers Employee in Django admin. Implements EmployeeViewSet with list, retrieve, update only — create and destroy are deferred to T3 and T4 which will wire to services. Adds URL routing in apps/hr/urls.py and includes it in vitali/urls.py. Adds apps.hr to TENANT_APPS in vitali/settings/base.py. NO create or destroy endpoints in this task — those flow through service-layer in T3/T4.
tasks.notes: shrimp-rules.md: English short app name. apps.hr NOT apps.recursos_humanos. EmployeeViewSet is intentionally INCOMPLETE — create and destroy are added in T3 and T4 via service-layer.

relatedFiles.description: Reference for Professional model + similar OneToOneField patterns

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. Create directory backend/apps/hr/ with __init__.py, apps.py (HRConfig with name='apps.hr'), models.py, admin.py, serializers.py, views.py, urls.py.
2. models.py: class Employee(models.Model) with OneToOneField(User, on_delete=CASCADE, related_name='employee'), hire_date=DateField(), employment_status=CharField(choices=EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_CHOICES, default='active'), contract_type=CharField(choices=CONTRACT_TYPE_CHOICES), terminated_at=DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True), created_at=auto_now_add, updated_at=auto_now. Add Meta with verbose_name 'Funcionário'.
3. Run python manage.py makemigrations hr to generate 0001_initial.py. Verify reversible (no destructive defaults).
4. apps/core/constants.py: add 'rh' to ALLOWED_MODULE_KEYS frozenset.
5. admin.py: @admin.register(Employee) class EmployeeAdmin with list_display, list_filter, search_fields.
6. serializers.py: EmployeeSerializer with fields user (read), full_name (source user.full_name), email (source user.email), role (source user.role.name), hire_date, employment_status, contract_type, terminated_at, created_at.
7. views.py: from rest_framework.viewsets import GenericViewSet; from rest_framework.mixins import ListModelMixin, RetrieveModelMixin, UpdateModelMixin. class EmployeeViewSet(GenericViewSet, ListModelMixin, RetrieveModelMixin, UpdateModelMixin) with queryset=Employee.objects.select_related('user__role'), serializer_class=EmployeeSerializer, permission_classes=[IsAuthenticated, HasPermission('admin')]. Filter terminated by default unless ?include_terminated=true.
8. urls.py: from rest_framework.routers import DefaultRouter; router.register('hr/employees', EmployeeViewSet, basename='employee'); urlpatterns=router.urls.
9. vitali/urls.py: add path('api/v1/', include('apps.hr.urls')) after the other api/v1/ includes.
10. vitali/settings/base.py: add 'apps.hr' to TENANT_APPS list.
tasks.verificationCriteria: python manage.py migrate runs cleanly. python manage.py shell -c "from apps.hr.models import Employee; print(Employee._meta.get_fields())" lists all expected fields. GET /api/v1/hr/employees/ returns 200 with empty list (no employees yet). 'rh' visible in tenant admin module list. ruff check apps/hr/ passes. mypy apps/hr/ --ignore-missing-imports passes.

tasks.summary: T1 complete (commit b06e763). Created apps.hr Django app with Employee model (UUID PK, OneToOneField to User, hire_date, employment_status, contract_type, terminated_at). Wired admin, serializers (read-only computed full_name/email/role), ViewSet (list/retrieve/update only — create/destroy correctly deferred), URL routing, TENANT_APPS. Added 'rh' to ALLOWED_MODULE_KEYS. Migration 0001_initial reversible. Tests pass (2/2 — TenantTestCase pattern matching codebase). ruff + mypy clean. Verified post-rebase still works.

tasks.name: T2: User.must_change_password field + UserInvitation model + migrations
tasks.description: Adds the must_change_password BooleanField (default=False) to User model and creates the UserInvitation model used by the invite-email auth flow. Two separate migrations to keep the diff bisectable. UserInvitation fields: id (UUID PK), user (FK to User), created_by (FK to User, the admin who sent it), token_hash (CharField for SHA256 of the JWT signature so the same token can be re-validated), expires_at (DateTimeField, default now+72h), consumed_at (DateTimeField, nullable). Indexed on token_hash for lookup.
tasks.notes: Two separate migrations on purpose — must_change_password is a User schema change, UserInvitation is a new table. Bisectable diff if anything goes wrong.
tasks.implementationGuide:
1. apps/core/models.py: add must_change_password=BooleanField(default=False) to User model. Place after is_active/is_staff fields.
2. python manage.py makemigrations core --name add_user_must_change_password.
3. apps/core/models.py: add new UserInvitation model. Fields: id=UUIDField(primary_key, default=uuid4), user=ForeignKey(User, on_delete=CASCADE, related_name='invitations'), created_by=ForeignKey(User, on_delete=SET_NULL, null=True, related_name='invitations_sent'), token_hash=CharField(max_length=64, db_index=True, unique=True), expires_at=DateTimeField(), consumed_at=DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True), created_at=auto_now_add. Add helper property is_expired (returns now() > expires_at), is_consumed (consumed_at is not None).
4. python manage.py makemigrations core --name add_user_invitation.
5. Verify both migrations are reversible. The must_change_password field defaults to False so existing rows are safe (no NOT NULL violation).
6. Add UserInvitation to apps/core/admin.py with read-only display.
tasks.verificationCriteria: Both migrations apply cleanly forward and backward (python manage.py migrate core <prev>). User.objects.first().must_change_password returns False for existing users. UserInvitation.objects.create(user=u, created_by=admin, token_hash='abc', expires_at=now()+timedelta(hours=72)) succeeds. ruff + mypy pass.

tasks.name: T3: EmployeeOnboardingService + EmployeeViewSet.create() + unit tests
tasks.description: The core cascade orchestrator. Implements service-layer pattern (NOT Django signals — locked decision 1A). The service runs an atomic block creating Employee, User (with must_change_password if mode 1/2), conditionally Professional (clinical roles), writes 3-4 AuditLog entries with shared correlation_id (locked decision 2A), and queues WhatsApp setup via transaction.on_commit (locked decision 1B fail-open). EmployeeViewSet.create() is a thin wrapper that deserializes the payload and calls the service. Unit tests cover all 3 auth modes, clinical/non-clinical roles, validation errors, WhatsApp opt-in, AuditLog chain integrity. WhatsApp Celery task is mocked at this layer — real-worker integration is T13.
tasks.notes: Mode=invite path is intentionally NotImplementedError until T6 lands. T6 imports a helper from T6's module that T3's service calls. T6 will modify this service to wire it up, OR we can pre-wire by creating a stub _create_invitation that T6 fills in. Recommend: T3 leaves the _create_invitation method as `raise NotImplementedError` and T6 implements it. This keeps T3 atomic and shippable independent of T6 (you can ship T3 with modes 1+2 working).
tasks.verificationCriteria: All 6+ unit tests pass. POST /api/v1/hr/employees/ with mode=admin_typed_password creates Employee + User + 2 AuditLog entries (no Professional). With clinical role: creates Professional + 3 AuditLog entries. AuditLog entries share the same correlation_id. WhatsApp opt-in queues the Celery task (mocked). Mode=invite raises NotImplementedError (will be fixed by T6). ruff + mypy pass.

tasks.name: T4: EmployeeDeactivationService + EmployeeViewSet.destroy() + unit tests
tasks.description: Implements F-15 cascade. Service soft-deletes Employee (status=terminated, terminated_at=now), sets User.is_active=False, iterates OutstandingToken for the user and blacklists each (idempotent for already-blacklisted), deactivates Professional if exists, marks WhatsApp staff channel inactive if exists. Writes AuditLog chain with new correlation_id (locked decision 2A). Hard-delete is NOT implemented (locked decision 1D — deferred to Sprint 19). Re-activate path: PATCH employment_status=active → User.is_active=True. EmployeeViewSet.destroy() calls the service, returns the soft-deleted Employee.
tasks.notes: Token blacklist iteration is bounded — typical user has <20 OutstandingToken rows. shrimp-rules.md: idempotent (already-blacklisted token doesn't crash the cascade).

relatedFiles.description: Existing logout flow with token.blacklist() at line 241

tasks.implementationGuide:
1. apps/hr/services.py: append EmployeeDeactivationService class.

class EmployeeDeactivationService:
    def deactivate(self, employee, *, requesting_user):
        from rest_framework_simplejwt.token_blacklist.models import OutstandingToken
        from rest_framework_simplejwt.tokens import RefreshToken
        from rest_framework_simplejwt.exceptions import TokenError
        correlation_id = str(uuid4())
        with transaction.atomic():
            employee.employment_status = 'terminated'
            employee.terminated_at = timezone.now()
            employee.save(update_fields=['employment_status', 'terminated_at'])
            employee.user.is_active = False
            employee.user.save(update_fields=['is_active'])
            tokens_revoked = 0
            for ot in OutstandingToken.objects.filter(user=employee.user):
                try:
                    RefreshToken(ot.token).blacklist()
                    tokens_revoked += 1
                except TokenError:
                    pass  # already blacklisted, ignore
            if hasattr(employee.user, 'professional'):
                employee.user.professional.is_active = False
                employee.user.professional.save(update_fields=['is_active'])
            self._audit_chain(employee, correlation_id, tokens_revoked, requesting_user)
        return employee
    
    def _audit_chain(self, employee, correlation_id, tokens_revoked, requesting_user):
        AuditLog.objects.create(user=requesting_user, action='employee_terminated', resource_type='employee', resource_id=str(employee.id), new_data={'correlation_id': correlation_id})
        AuditLog.objects.create(user=requesting_user, action='tokens_revoked', resource_type='user', resource_id=str(employee.user.id), new_data={'correlation_id': correlation_id, 'count': tokens_revoked})
        if hasattr(employee.user, 'professional'):
            AuditLog.objects.create(user=requesting_user, action='professional_deactivated', resource_type='professional', resource_id=str(employee.user.professional.id), new_data={'correlation_id': correlation_id})

2. apps/hr/views.py: add destroy() method to EmployeeViewSet:
    def destroy(self, request, pk=None):
        employee = self.get_object()
        service = EmployeeDeactivationService()
        employee = service.deactivate(employee, requesting_user=request.user)
        return Response(EmployeeSerializer(employee).data, status=200)

Also add reactivate path: PATCH employment_status='active' in update() should also flip User.is_active back to True. Add hook in serializer or in EmployeeViewSet.partial_update().

3. apps/hr/tests/test_services_deactivation.py (NEW): test_deactivate_admin_no_professional, test_deactivate_clinical_with_professional (asserts Professional.is_active=False), test_token_blacklist_idempotent (creates 3 OutstandingToken rows, blacklists 1 of them manually first, runs deactivate, asserts no error and remaining 2 also blacklisted), test_audit_log_chain_correlation, test_reactivate_flips_user_is_active.
tasks.verificationCriteria: DELETE /api/v1/hr/employees/{id}/ soft-deletes (status=terminated, User.is_active=False). All OutstandingToken rows for user are blacklisted (next refresh attempt → 401). Professional.is_active=False if exists. AuditLog chain has 2-3 entries with shared correlation_id. Reactivate via PATCH flips back. All 5+ unit tests pass.

tasks.name: T5: PasswordChangeRequiredMiddleware + MIDDLEWARE wiring + unit tests
tasks.description: New middleware that mirrors the existing MFARequiredMiddleware pattern (locked decision 1C). On every authenticated request, if request.user.must_change_password is True AND request.path is NOT in the allowlist, return 403 with the redirect payload. Insert in MIDDLEWARE list AFTER MFARequiredMiddleware. Allowlist: /api/v1/auth/password, /api/v1/auth/logout, /api/v1/me. Unit tests cover all branches.
tasks.notes: Middleware ORDER matters: must come AFTER AuthenticationMiddleware (so request.user is set) and AFTER MFARequiredMiddleware (so MFA gate runs first — logical ordering: complete MFA, THEN change password).
tasks.verificationCriteria: User with must_change_password=True hitting GET /api/v1/encounters/ → 403 with redirect payload. Same user hitting GET /api/v1/me → 200. Same user hitting POST /api/v1/auth/password with valid new password → 200, flag cleared, subsequent requests succeed. Anonymous user → no middleware action. All 5+ unit tests pass.

Each task ships its own commit and includes its tests bundled per shrimp-rules.md convention. tasks.summary: T5 complete (commit 8de813f). PasswordChangeRequiredMiddleware mirrors MFARequiredMiddleware shape. Allowlist /auth/password, /auth/logout, /me. Non-allowlisted authenticated request with must_change_password=True returns 403 with PASSWORD_CHANGE_REQUIRED + redirect payload. Wired in MIDDLEWARE after MFARequiredMiddleware. ChangePasswordView clears the flag on success. 10 unit + integration tests pass (5 unit covering allowlist/no-flag/unauth/no-user; 5 integration with real JWT incl change-password-clears-flag end-to-end). 88/88 core test suite passes (10 new + 78 baseline). Notes: required JWT resolution helper since DRF auth fires in view layer not middleware — without it Bearer-token requests would bypass the gate. Used spec response shape (richer redirect field) over MFARequiredMiddleware shape since frontend interceptor depends on the redirect.

tasks.name: T6: UserInvitationView + SetPasswordView + email template + EmailService method + tests
tasks.description: Implements the third auth onboarding mode: send invitation email with token. New endpoints: POST /api/v1/auth/invite/ (admin-only, creates UserInvitation + signed JWT token + sends email) and POST /api/v1/auth/set-password/<token>/ (validates token + sets password + marks consumed + clears must_change_password + returns access JWT). New email template apps/core/templates/email/user_invitation.html. Adds EmailService.send_user_invitation method. Wires the previously-NotImplementedError invite path in EmployeeOnboardingService (T3) by importing and calling the invitation creation helper.
tasks.notes: shrimp-rules.md: token must be SINGLE-USE (consumed_at non-null after first set-password). Hash the token for storage so leaked DB doesn't expose valid tokens.
tasks.verificationCriteria: POST /api/v1/auth/invite/ (admin) creates UserInvitation + sends email (mocked). POST /api/v1/auth/set-password/<valid_token>/ with new password → 200 + access JWT + UserInvitation.consumed_at set. Expired token → 410. Already-consumed token → 400. Tampered token → 400. EmployeeOnboardingService with auth_mode='invite' now succeeds (no longer NotImplementedError) and creates invitation. All tests pass.

tasks.name: T7: setup_staff_whatsapp_channel Celery task + retry/Sentry + unit tests
tasks.description: The Celery task that EmployeeOnboardingService.onboard enqueues via transaction.on_commit. Sets up a WhatsApp staff channel for the new user via the Evolution API gateway. Implements the fail-open contract from locked decision 1B: 3x retry with exponential backoff, and on persistent failure writes AuditLog 'whatsapp_setup_failed' + logs to Sentry. Crucially: the task NEVER affects the User/Employee/Professional rows — its failure is contained.
tasks.notes: This task is mocked by T3's unit tests. Its actual behavior is verified by T7 unit tests + T13 integration test (real Celery worker, mocked Evolution API).

relatedFiles.description: Add setup_staff_channel method if not present

tasks.verificationCriteria: Task runs in eager mode for tests. Success path writes whatsapp_channel_created AuditLog. No-phone case skips silently. Transient error retries 3x. Persistent error writes whatsapp_setup_failed AuditLog (so the failure is visible in audit + Sentry catches it). All 4 tests pass.

tasks.name: T8: AddEmployeeModal 3-step component + Vitest tests
tasks.description: Frontend modal component with 3 steps (personal data / role + employment / auth choice). Conditional rendering for clinical role fields. Random password generator (secure 16-char). Vitali visual conventions. Vitest tests for conditional rendering, generator entropy, validation behavior.
tasks.notes: shrimp-rules.md: plain useState (no react-hook-form). Visual: slate-200 borders, blue-600 primary, rounded-xl cards. Reference modal pattern: /farmacia/catalog/page.tsx DrugForm. CPF mask: check frontend/lib/ for existing helper, otherwise add formatCPF in lib/formatters.ts.
tasks.implementationGuide:
1. frontend/components/hr/AddEmployeeModal.tsx (NEW): React component with useState for the 3-step state machine, payload accumulator, validation per step. Step 1: full_name, email, cpf (use existing CPF mask if available, else add helper), phone (optional). Step 2: role select (admin/medico/enfermeiro/recepcao/faturista/farmaceutico/dentista), hire_date (input type=date), contract_type select, employment_status select. If role in CLINICAL_ROLES: show council_type/number/state/specialty fields. Step 3: 3-radio: 'Definir senha temporária' (text input + helper text 'Funcionário deverá alterar no primeiro login'), 'Gerar senha aleatória' (button generates 16-char + shown in monospace + copy button), 'Enviar convite por email' (just info text). Sub-checkbox 'Configurar WhatsApp para comunicação interna' enabled only if Step 1 phone is filled.

2. Validation: clinical role + missing council fields → block Step 2 next button. Step 3 must select an auth mode.

3. On submit: POST /api/v1/hr/employees/ with the accumulated payload. On success: success toast listing side effects (User criado ✓, Professional criado ✓ if applicable, WhatsApp na fila ✓ if applicable). On error: inline error display.

4. Random password generator: use crypto.getRandomValues + a charset of A-Z a-z 0-9 + symbols (excluding visually-confusing ones like 0/O/l/1). 16 chars.

5. frontend/components/hr/AddEmployeeModal.test.tsx (NEW): Vitest tests using @testing-library/react. test_step_1_personal_fields_render, test_step_2_clinical_fields_show_on_role_change, test_step_2_blocks_next_with_missing_council, test_step_3_random_password_button_generates_secure_string, test_step_3_invite_mode_no_password_field, test_whatsapp_checkbox_disabled_without_phone, test_submit_calls_api_with_payload (mock fetch).
tasks.verificationCriteria: Modal renders all 3 steps. Clinical role conditionally shows council fields. Password generator produces 16-char string with diverse charset. WhatsApp checkbox disabled without phone. Submit POSTs correct payload shape. tsc + next lint clean. All 7+ Vitest tests pass.

tasks.name: T10: /auth/change-password forced page + Vitest test
tasks.description: Full-screen page (no dashboard chrome) that users land on when middleware T5 returns the PASSWORD_CHANGE_REQUIRED redirect. Form: current password, new password, confirm new password. Submits to POST /api/v1/auth/password (existing endpoint, modified by T5 to clear must_change_password). On success: redirect to /dashboard.
tasks.notes: Layout: should NOT include the dashboard nav (this is a forced flow, no escape). Use a layout.tsx in /auth/ that strips navigation OR rely on the route group pattern.
tasks.implementationGuide:

2. Validation: new === confirm; new is not the temp password (server enforces; client just confirms match); min 8 chars.

3. Submit: POST /api/v1/auth/password with {current_password, new_password}. On 200: localStorage.setItem('vitali_password_changed', 'true'); router.push('/dashboard'). On 400: show error inline.

4. frontend/app/auth/change-password/page.test.tsx (NEW): Vitest tests for password match validation, submit calls API, success redirects.
tasks.verificationCriteria: Page renders without dashboard chrome. Form validates client-side. Submit calls /api/v1/auth/password. Success redirects to /dashboard. tsc + next lint clean.

tasks.name: T12: Frontend axios interceptor for PASSWORD_CHANGE_REQUIRED redirect + tests
tasks.description: Catches 403 responses with code=PASSWORD_CHANGE_REQUIRED from any API call and redirects the SPA to /auth/change-password. Implemented as a fetch wrapper or axios interceptor. Tests cover the redirect behavior.
tasks.notes: Don't migrate ALL fetch calls in this task — that's a big diff. Migrate the dashboard shell + 2-3 representative pages. Capture remaining migrations as a TODO for Sprint 19.
tasks.implementationGuide:
1. frontend/lib/auth.ts (or new frontend/lib/api.ts): create a wrapper around fetch that intercepts 403 responses with body.error.code === 'PASSWORD_CHANGE_REQUIRED'. On match: redirect via window.location.href = body.error.redirect (or use Next.js router if in component context).

2. Update existing fetch usages in components to use the wrapper. OR: add a global error boundary in dashboard layout that handles the 403.

3. Recommended approach: create lib/api.ts with apiFetch(path, options) that wraps fetch + handles auth errors uniformly (token refresh, password-change redirect, MFA redirect). Migrate existing fetch calls gradually OR ship the wrapper alongside one or two demo migrations.

4. frontend/lib/api.test.ts (NEW): Vitest tests for redirect on PASSWORD_CHANGE_REQUIRED, normal pass-through for other responses, JWT auto-refresh on 401.
tasks.verificationCriteria: apiFetch returns normally for 200 responses. apiFetch redirects via window.location for 403 PASSWORD_CHANGE_REQUIRED. tsc + next lint clean.

tasks.name: T13: CRITICAL — WhatsApp fail-open integration test (real Celery worker)
tasks.description: The cornerstone test that proves the cascade architecture. Real Celery worker (CELERY_TASK_ALWAYS_EAGER=False with eager_propagates_exceptions=False), mocked Evolution API gateway that raises ConnectionError. Asserts that despite the WhatsApp failure, the Employee, User, Professional rows ALL persist in the database, and the AuditLog 'whatsapp_setup_failed' entry is written. This test is REQUIRED for the sprint to ship — it is the architectural correctness gate.
tasks.notes: This is THE test that the sprint hinges on. If this test fails, the cascade architecture is wrong. Run it locally before pushing. Run it in CI on every commit. Add to required CI checks.
tasks.implementationGuide:
1. backend/apps/hr/tests/test_integration_whatsapp_failopen.py (NEW). Use TenantTestCase (or whatever the project uses for integration tests with celery + db). Override settings to enable real Celery: @override_settings(CELERY_TASK_ALWAYS_EAGER=True, CELERY_TASK_EAGER_PROPAGATES=False) — actually for true integration we want eager=True with propagates=False (so the cascade catches the exception inside the task, not raised back to caller). Alternatively: spin up a real Celery worker via celery.contrib.testing.worker.

2. Test:
    @override_settings(CELERY_TASK_ALWAYS_EAGER=True, CELERY_TASK_EAGER_PROPAGATES=False)
    def test_whatsapp_failure_does_not_roll_back_employee(self):
        # Enable WhatsApp module for tenant
        FeatureFlag.objects.create(tenant=self.tenant, module_key='whatsapp', is_enabled=True)
        # Mock the gateway to fail
        with patch('apps.whatsapp.gateway.EvolutionAPIGateway.setup_staff_channel', side_effect=ConnectionError('Evolution API down')):
            payload = {'full_name': 'Dr. Test', 'email': 'test@vitali.com', 'cpf': '111.111.111-11', 'phone': '+5511999999999', 'role': 'medico', 'hire_date': '2026-04-26', 'contract_type': 'clt', 'employment_status': 'active', 'council_type': 'CRM', 'council_number': '12345', 'council_state': 'SP', 'specialty': 'Clínica Médica', 'auth_mode': 'random_password', 'password': 'GeneratedTest123!', 'setup_whatsapp': True}
            response = self.client.post('/api/v1/hr/employees/', payload, format='json')
        # Despite WhatsApp failure, all DB rows persist
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 201)
        self.assertTrue(Employee.objects.filter(user__email='test@vitali.com').exists())
        self.assertTrue(User.objects.filter(email='test@vitali.com').exists())
        self.assertTrue(Professional.objects.filter(user__email='test@vitali.com').exists())
        # AuditLog records the failure (max retries exceeded)
        # Wait briefly for the eager task to run (or use celery sync)
        failed_audit = AuditLog.objects.filter(action='whatsapp_setup_failed').first()
        self.assertIsNotNone(failed_audit)
        self.assertIn('correlation_id', failed_audit.new_data)

3. Variation: test_whatsapp_intermittent_then_succeeds — gateway raises 2x then succeeds on 3rd retry. Asserts whatsapp_channel_created AuditLog written, no whatsapp_setup_failed.
tasks.verificationCriteria: Test passes with mocked gateway raising ConnectionError + Employee/User/Professional all persist + whatsapp_setup_failed AuditLog present + correlation_id chain intact. Test takes <60s. ruff + mypy clean.

tasks.name: T14: E2E Playwright — hire-a-doctor + invite-email flows
tasks.description: End-to-end Playwright tests that walk through the full user journeys. Test 1: admin clicks Adicionar Funcionário → fills 3-step modal with clinical role + random_password mode → submits → sees success toast → navigates to /configuracoes/profissionais and verifies new doctor appears. Test 2: admin uses invite mode → email is intercepted (or queued for inspection) → simulates user clicking link → set-password page → enters password → lands on dashboard authenticated.

5. Add to CI workflow: new GitHub Actions job 'frontend-e2e' that runs these.
